title: Hangover-Cure Peruvian Pilchards Stir-fry
description: A fresh and dense Peruvian dish featuring pilchards that can be used as a hangover cure.

ingredients.name: Pilchards
ingredients.notes: Fresh pilchards can be found at Asian markets or online.

ingredients.name: Jalea (coarsely ground chile paste)
ingredients.notes: A must for the Peruvian cuisine, available at Latin American grocery stores or online.

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Fresh is best but you can use bottled if fresh unavailable

instructions: Wash and pat dry the pilchards.
In a bowl, combine the pilchards with lime juice, salt, pepper, and olive oil. Toss until evenly coated.
Heat a tablespoon of olive oil in a wok or large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped garlic and sauté for 30 seconds to one minute, until fragrant.
Squeeze in the remaining lime juice from the pilchards mixture into the pan, allowing it to cook down slightly with the garlic.
Gently add the pilchards back into the wok or skillet. Cook them gently without stirring too much for about 3-4 minutes so they get crispy on the outside and still juicy on the inside.
Add finely chopped onion over the pilchards, mix well to combine.
Drizzle in jalea over the stir-fry mixture and continue to cook until fully combined and the dish is heated through.
Serve hot with a side of rice or naan bread.
